ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for host revocation: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover host revocation, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the disaster-recovery pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for host revocation.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for host revocation.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Fleet Management Control Plane host revocation inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: host-revocation_disaster-recovery_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for host revocation disaster-recovery that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for host revocation; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for host revocation.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for host revocation recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for host revocation.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for host revocation.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing host revocation data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling host revocation inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for host revocation.
acceptance_criteria: Given host revocation data exists, when the disaster-recovery inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: host revocation connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
